reasoning: Given the current observation, there is a linear relationship between A, B, and Y. However, since changing A does not seem to affect Y in the current observation, I will try to observe the effect of changing B.

Y appears to depend only on A, as changing A affects Y and B does not. To confirm the relationship, I will decrease A again.

The relationship between A and Y appears consistent with a linear relationship, where Y depends solely on A. To confirm the slope and intercept, I will perform a linear regression on the observed data.
final_equation: Y = m*A + b, where m = (Y2 - Y1) / (A2 - A1) and b = Y1 - m*A1
